mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are positive in >55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals an imminent labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer and investment demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production reflects robust manufacturing and utility output, which translates to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when the yield curve is inverted, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate ongoing labor market expansion and rising aggregate income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a rolling Rank IC below 0.01 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above the threshold do not lead to negative 5-day returns in >65% of historical instances, or the signal is neutralized by simultaneous fiscal stimulus.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of strong quarterly GDP growth and low federal funds rates creates an optimal macro environment where robust economic expansion is not offset by high discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when inflation exceeds 3.0%, or the joint signal produces negative returns in >50% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment rates during recessionary periods signal prolonged economic contraction and falling corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: High unemployment during recessions fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or the signal becomes positive due to aggressive monetary accommodation.
